The health benefits of Dracula species are largely anecdotal, but they are said to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiviral properties. The bioactive constituents of Dracula species include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids. The most common herbal preparations made from Dracula species are teas, infusions, and tinctures, although these are not widely available or recommended for medicinal use.
